title: Guidelines for Patternoid Feature Extraction
author: Kurzbach, Sophie
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository contains the complete set of code scripts, Fiji macros, and pipelines used for the image preprocessing, segmentation, and feature extraction described in the dissertation  "Modeling Tumor Invasion in Engineered Microenvironments: Toward Personalized Drug Response Testing"  by Sophie Kurzbach. 
 The tools enable the extraction of geometric and intensity-based features from 3D patterned organoids ("patternoids") and are intended to ensure methodological reproducibility and support further adaptation for related applications in organoid image analysis. 
  Contents:  
   🔬      Nucleus Segmentation and Tracking Pipelines (Arivis Vision4D) (version 4.22)    
 
 
  Cell_segmentation.pipeline  – nucleus segmentation using Cellpose 
 
 
  Cell_segmentation_Tracking.pipeline  – segmentation and cell tracking 
 
 
   
   📐      Patternoid Invasion Assay Analysis     Preprocessing:  
 
 
  Zproj_from_.lif.py  – extraction of maximum intensity projections from LIF z-stacks 
 
 
  Alignment.ijm  – alignment of patternoid masks based on shared ROI 
 
 
 With user manual:  Alignment_README.txt  
 
 
 
 
  Feature Extraction:  
 
 
  InvD.ijm  – quantification of maximum invasive distance 
 
 
 With user manual:  InvD_README.txt  
 
 
 
 
 Each script and macro is provided with inline documentation and accompanying usage guides where applicable. Users are encouraged to cite this repository and the dissertation when using or adapting these tools.
